zotero.abstractNote: Kernel methods are a cornerstone of machine learning, but their computational cost is a major bottleneck. For a dataset of size n, they typically require O(nˆ3) time and O(nˆ2) memory. We propose a method to solve the kernel linear system using the conjugate gradient (CG) method, which reduces the complexity to O(nˆ2) time and O(n) memory. Our approach is based on a novel approximation of the kernel matrix-vector product, which we derive using a low-rank approximation of the kernel matrix. We show that our method is competitive with state-of-the-art approaches in terms of accuracy and significantly faster in terms of training time.
